strategy: Hit with Storm (+25%), avoid Myth (55% resist). He is low health (4,500) but layered in shields, so race him.
He opens with a -99% Tower Shield and re-shields when single-target hits break it. Attack with all-enemy AoE spells, which do not trigger the re-shield, or Melt to dispel.
If you Pierce or Steal his shield, next round he puts up an Aegis-protected -100% shield of your school, so use off-school AoE or Melt rather than piercing.
Do not blade (he Disarms the receiver) and do not single-trap him (he Cleanse-Wards one trap off), use multi-trap spells or Indemnity traps.
Every 5 rounds he plants a 100,000 Shadow bomb that detonates two rounds later, which is lethal, so burst him dead before Round 7 with AoE Storm damage.
cheatNotes: He starts with a -99% Tower Shield, a single-target hit that breaks it just makes him re-shield.
Single-target damage that breaks the shield triggers a fresh shield, do not chip him single-target.
All-enemy AoE damage does NOT trigger the re-shield, this is how you hit him.
He will not re-shield if you remove the -99% shield while he already has another shield up.
Melt dispels the shield cheat outright.
Pierce or Steal Ward on him sets up the next cheat.
The round after you Pierce or Steal, he puts up an Aegis-protected -100% shield of your school, so avoid piercing and use off-school AoE.
Try to remove that school shield and he recasts it, leave it and hit off-school.
Cast a blade and he Disarms it off the receiver, skip blades.
Single-trap him and he Cleanse-Wards one trap off, use multi-trap spells or Indemnity.
Multi-trap spells (Elemental Trap) and Indemnity traps beat his single-trap removal.
Every 5 rounds he plants a 100,000 Shadow bomb that detonates two rounds later, kill him before it lands (before Round 7).
